free_text: Measurement of residual cross sections in the 7Li + 89Y
reaction up to 5.7 MeV/nucleon and analysis of fusion
mechanisms
Same cross sections given. TTYs also given.
14UD BARC-TIFR Pelletron facility
Self-supporting thin foils of 89Y and 27Al
(purity 99.99%) having thickness ~2-3.9 mg/cm2 and
~1.5-3.0 mg/cm2, respectively, were prepared by proper
rolling technique.
The foil 27Al used as a recoil catcher and energy
degrader.
The energy resolution of the detector was 2.0
at 1332 keV gamma-rays of 60Co.
gamma-ray spectrum of the 39.4 MeV 7Li
irradiated 89Y collected 49 min.
The average charge ~456 uC was collected for each set
of irradiation.
Total charge measured by an electron-suppressed
Faraday cup installed behind the target stack.
Including error in the SRIM calculation and
target thickness determination.
The 95% confidence limit reported by the
authors had been divided by two.
The total uncertainty in the cross section are:
- Uncertainty in the estimation of beam energy
due to degradation of energy successive
target-catcher foil.
- Energy straggling is expected to be small.
target thickness (~2%)
geometry dependent efficiency of the
detector (<= 2%)
incident beam flux  (~6-7%)
dead time of the detector (<= 10%)
VT
